meta.purpose: Track coverage for frequently overlooked post-discharge items across all 9 B-1/B-2 plans. Many travelers assume hospital coverage implies follow-up coverage — it often does not.
meta.disclaimer: Evidence-based only. Items without Certificate-level evidence are flagged NEEDS MANUAL REVIEW. Never use for claims advice.

coverage_key.COVERED_URC: Covered at actual/usual-reasonable-customary cost up to plan maximum, subject to deductible and coinsurance
coverage_key.COVERED_CAPPED: Covered but subject to a dollar or visit cap — payout may fall far below actual cost
coverage_key.EXCLUDED: Explicitly excluded from coverage — no payout regardless of medical necessity
coverage_key.NEEDS_MANUAL_REVIEW: No Certificate-level evidence found — cannot determine coverage without full policy document review
coverage_key.NOT_APPLICABLE: Not a benefit category for this plan type

physical_therapy.detail: Physical Therapy covered at 100% in-network / 90% out-of-network / 100% international. Requires medical order or treatment plan. No visit cap confirmed in Certificate.
physical_therapy.flag: No visit cap = significant advantage over competitors. Most other plans cap at 10 visits or $500 total.

occupational_therapy.detail: Not explicitly mentioned in Certificate evidence. May be covered under eligible medical expenses (Ancillary Services language) but no explicit OT benefit found.
occupational_therapy.flag: NEEDS MANUAL REVIEW — check Certificate for OT language or ancillary services inclusion

speech_therapy.detail: Not explicitly mentioned in Certificate evidence. May fall under eligible medical expenses but no explicit Speech Therapy benefit confirmed.

home_nursing_care.detail: Home Nursing Care covered at 100% in-network / 90% out-of-network. Must be provided by a Home Health Care Agency. Requires direct transfer from an acute care facility.
home_nursing_care.precert_note: Pre-certification REQUIRED. Failure to pre-certify = 50% reduction of eligible expenses.
home_nursing_care.flag: Direct transfer from acute care required — cannot start home nursing independently. Pre-certify immediately.

home_health_aide.detail: Home Health Care Agency is mentioned as provider for Home Nursing Care but Home Health Aide as a separate benefit is not explicitly confirmed.

rehabilitation_facility.detail: Extended Care Facility is listed as a pre-certification-required service, suggesting it may be covered, but no explicit benefit amount or coverage confirmation found in evidence.
rehabilitation_facility.flag: Extended Care Facility pre-certification required — implies coverage exists. NEEDS MANUAL REVIEW for benefit details.

durable_medical_equipment.detail: Durable Medical Equipment (DME) explicitly covered at 100% in-network / 90% out-of-network / 100% international. Includes walkers, wheelchairs, crutches, and other medically necessary equipment.
durable_medical_equipment.flag: Strong DME coverage — no dollar cap confirmed. Significant advantage over most competitor plans.

prescription_refills_outpatient.detail: Prescriptions covered. In-network: covered under eligible medical expenses (no separate Rx billing line). Out-of-network: 90%. Maximum 90-day supply per prescription. If plan max is $500K or $1M: Rx sub-limit $250,000 per Period of Coverage.
prescription_refills_outpatient.flag: In-network Rx billing is bundled — traveler does not need to file separate Rx claims when using in-network pharmacy.

follow_up_specialist_visits.detail: Outpatient specialist visits covered as eligible medical expenses. In-network: 100%. Out-of-network: 90%. No visit cap for specialist follow-ups identified.

follow_up_imaging.detail: Follow-up MRI, CT, X-ray covered as eligible medical expenses. In-network: 100%. Out-of-network: 90%. No imaging sub-limit confirmed.

strengths: PT covered with no visit cap — best in class
DME covered with no dollar cap
Home Nursing covered (with pre-cert and acute-transfer requirement)
weaknesses: OT and Speech Therapy not confirmed — needs Certificate review
Home Nursing requires direct acute care transfer — cannot use independently
Pre-certification required for Home Nursing and Extended Care or face 50% penalty

physical_therapy.detail: Physical Therapy covered at 100% everywhere. Requires medical order or treatment plan. No visit cap confirmed.
physical_therapy.flag: 100% coinsurance everywhere — no in-network vs OON distinction. Best PT coverage.

occupational_therapy.detail: Not explicitly confirmed in Certificate evidence.

speech_therapy.detail: Not explicitly confirmed in Certificate evidence.

home_nursing_care.detail: Home Nursing Care covered at 100%. Provided by Home Health Care Agency. Direct transfer from acute care facility required.

home_health_aide.detail: Not explicitly confirmed as a separate benefit.

rehabilitation_facility.detail: Extended Care Facility pre-certification required (implies covered). Explicit benefit details not in evidence.

durable_medical_equipment.detail: DME covered at 100% everywhere. No dollar cap confirmed.
durable_medical_equipment.flag: Best DME coverage — 100% with no dollar cap.

prescription_refills_outpatient.detail: Prescriptions covered at 100%. 90-day supply max. If plan max ≥$1M: Rx sub-limit = $250,000. If plan max is $20K or $100K: covered up to plan max.

follow_up_specialist_visits.detail: 100% coinsurance everywhere. No visit cap confirmed.

follow_up_imaging.detail: 100% coinsurance everywhere. No imaging sub-limit confirmed.

strengths: 100% everywhere — no OON penalty on any post-discharge service
weaknesses: OT and Speech not confirmed
Home Nursing requires acute transfer + pre-cert

physical_therapy.detail: Physical Therapy covered at 100% in-network / 80% out-of-network. Requires medical order or treatment plan. No visit cap confirmed in Certificate.

home_nursing_care.detail: Not explicitly confirmed in Certificate evidence for CoverAmerica Gold.
home_nursing_care.flag: NEEDS MANUAL REVIEW — full Certificate needed.

durable_medical_equipment.detail: Not explicitly confirmed in Certificate evidence.

prescription_refills_outpatient.detail: Rx covered. In-network: not applicable (bundled). Out-of-network: 80%. 90-day maximum supply.
prescription_refills_outpatient.flag: 80% OON coinsurance means 20% patient OOP on outpatient Rx — weaker than IMG plans.

strengths: PT covered with no confirmed visit cap
weaknesses: Home Nursing, DME, Rehab not confirmed — needs Certificate review
80% OON coinsurance on all post-discharge services

physical_therapy.detail: Physiotherapy / Physical Medicine: $50 per visit, 1 visit per day, 10 visits maximum per Period of Insurance. Must be prescribed by a Physician. Total maximum: $500.
physical_therapy.flag: SIGNIFICANTLY CAPPED — average PT visit billed at $100-$200. Plan pays $50/visit leaving patient responsible for $50-$150 per visit. $500 total cap exhausted after 10 visits.

occupational_therapy.detail: Not explicitly mentioned — check whether included in Physiotherapy/Physical Medicine language.

home_nursing_care.detail: Not found in extracted Certificate evidence.

durable_medical_equipment.detail: Not explicitly confirmed in Certificate evidence.

prescription_refills_outpatient.detail: Prescriptions covered. If plan max ≤$250K: up to full plan max. If plan max $500K or $1M: Rx sub-limit $250,000. 90-day maximum supply. 100% coinsurance everywhere.
prescription_refills_outpatient.flag: 100% coinsurance on Rx is a strength — no OON penalty.

follow_up_specialist_visits.detail: 100% coinsurance everywhere. No visit cap confirmed.

strengths: 100% coinsurance on Rx and follow-up visits
weaknesses: PT capped at $500 / 10 visits — below real-world costs
Home Nursing, DME, Rehab not confirmed

plans.plan_type_warning: SCHEDULED BENEFIT PLAN — all payouts are fixed dollar amounts, not percentage of actual cost. Post-discharge benefits are particularly limited.

physical_therapy.detail: Physical Therapy: $40 per visit, 1 visit per day, maximum 12 visits per period. Fixed benefit — plan pays $40 regardless of actual visit cost ($100-$200 typical). Total maximum: $480.
physical_therapy.flag: SCHEDULED BENEFIT — $40/visit vs $100-$200 actual cost. Plan pays less than half actual cost. Patient owes $60-$160 per visit after plan payment.

occupational_therapy.detail: Not in evidence. Likely excluded or not a listed scheduled benefit.

home_nursing_care.detail: Not confirmed in evidence. Scheduled benefit plan may have a fixed daily amount if covered.

durable_medical_equipment.detail: Not in evidence. If covered, would be a fixed scheduled amount.

prescription_refills_outpatient.detail: Outpatient Prescription Drugs: Plan A $150 / Plan B $200 / Plan C $250 / Plan D $300 per period of insurance. Fixed dollar maximum — not percentage-based. Any Rx costs above this limit are entirely patient responsibility.
prescription_refills_outpatient.flag: CRITICAL WEAKNESS — $150-$300 total outpatient Rx for entire trip. A single specialty medication can exceed this in 1-2 fills. Severely inadequate for post-hospitalization Rx.

follow_up_specialist_visits.detail: Specialist visits likely covered at fixed scheduled benefit (see physician office visit schedule). Actual cost may far exceed fixed benefit.
follow_up_specialist_visits.flag: All services are fixed scheduled amounts — follow-up specialist bills $200-$500; plan likely pays $50-$100 fixed.

follow_up_imaging.detail: Follow-up MRI/CT: fixed scheduled benefit amount applies. Actual MRI cost $500-$3,000; plan scheduled amount likely far below.
follow_up_imaging.flag: MRI actual cost $500-$3,000; VisitorSecure scheduled benefit severely underpays imaging.

strengths: Has PT and Rx benefits (unlike SC Basic which excludes PT)
weaknesses: SCHEDULED BENEFIT — every post-discharge item pays fixed low dollar amounts
Rx cap $150-$300 total is dangerously low for post-hospitalization medication
PT pays only $40/visit ($480 max) vs $100-$200 actual
Follow-up imaging severely underpaid at scheduled rates

plans.source_document: Coverage Summary / Brochure (L4/L5) — full Certificate not obtained
plans.data_quality_warning: NEEDS MANUAL REVIEW across all categories — full Certificate of Insurance not yet obtained. All data below from brochure-level sources only.

strengths: UHC PPO network (strong access to in-network providers for post-discharge care)
weaknesses: All post-discharge items unconfirmed — full Certificate required

physical_therapy.detail: Physiotherapy: $50 per visit, up to a maximum of $500 total (10 visits at $50). Must be prescribed by physician.
physical_therapy.flag: Same low cap as Safe Travels ($500 total). Far below real PT costs.

occupational_therapy.detail: Not confirmed in Policy Wording evidence.

home_nursing_care.detail: Not explicitly confirmed in Policy Wording.

durable_medical_equipment.detail: Hospital ancillary services ($20K sub-limit) includes ER and OR use but DME as a separate outpatient benefit is not confirmed.

prescription_refills_outpatient.detail: Prescription Drugs: $500 maximum (confirmed under pre-existing sub-limits). For new-condition Rx, a separate general Rx benefit may exist at a higher limit — not confirmed in extracted evidence.
prescription_refills_outpatient.flag: AMBIGUOUS — $500 Rx confirmed for pre-existing conditions. New-condition Rx limit requires Policy Wording review. If general Rx limit is also $500, this is severely inadequate for post-hospitalization Rx.

follow_up_specialist_visits.detail: Outpatient visits covered at 80-90% in-network / 60-70% OON as eligible medical expenses. No separate visit cap for follow-up confirmed.
follow_up_specialist_visits.flag: OON exposure 30-40% — significantly worse than competitor plans for out-of-network follow-up care.

follow_up_imaging.detail: Hospital ancillary $20K sub-limit covers imaging performed in hospital. Outpatient follow-up imaging (standalone radiology center) — covered at 80-90% in-network but no separate imaging sub-limit confirmed.

strengths: Outpatient specialist visits covered at 80-90% in-network
weaknesses: PT capped at $500 total
Rx ambiguous — $500 confirmed only for pre-ex; new-condition Rx limit not confirmed
OON penalty: 30-40% patient exposure on all post-discharge services
OT, Speech, Home Nursing, DME, Rehab all unconfirmed — needs Policy Wording review
CANCER EXCLUDED — post-discharge follow-up after cancer diagnosis entirely denied

plans.carrier: Crum & Forster SPC via ITI SP / Seven Corners

physical_therapy.detail: Physiotherapy and Chiropractic Care are EXPLICITLY EXCLUDED. Exact language: 'You are not covered for Physiotherapy or Chiropractic Care.'
physical_therapy.flag: CRITICAL — complete exclusion. Any PT required after injury or surgery = 100% patient cost. Choose Choice over Basic if PT is a concern.

occupational_therapy.detail: Not addressed in Certificate. Given PT/physio exclusion, OT may also be excluded.
occupational_therapy.flag: High likelihood of exclusion given PT is excluded — verify.

home_nursing_care.detail: Not explicitly confirmed in Certificate evidence.

prescription_refills_outpatient.detail: Prescription Drugs: URC up to Medical Maximum. No separate Rx sub-limit — treated same as eligible medical expenses. Strong Rx coverage.
prescription_refills_outpatient.flag: STRENGTH — no Rx sub-limit. Prescriptions covered at URC up to full plan maximum.

follow_up_specialist_visits.detail: Physician Office Visits: $30 copay, then URC up to Medical Maximum. 100% in-network. 80% OON up to $2,000 OOP max then 100%.

follow_up_imaging.detail: Covered as eligible medical expenses. Same coinsurance as other outpatient services. No imaging sub-limit.

strengths: Rx covered at URC with no sub-limit — best in class
weaknesses: ZERO PT coverage — explicit exclusion
OT likely also excluded
Home Nursing, DME, Rehab unconfirmed
Cannot renew beyond 364 days — forces plan switch for long-stay visitors

plans.carrier: Crum & Forster SPC via ITI SP / Seven Corners

physical_therapy.detail: Physiotherapy and Chiropractic Care: $50 per visit, 10 visits maximum ($500 total). Must be prescribed by physician and administered by physical therapist.
physical_therapy.flag: KEY UPGRADE over Basic (which excludes PT entirely). Cap of $500 is still low vs real costs.

occupational_therapy.detail: Not explicitly confirmed. PT/Physio language does not mention OT.

prescription_refills_outpatient.detail: Prescription Drugs: URC up to Medical Maximum. No separate Rx sub-limit. Identical to Basic.
prescription_refills_outpatient.flag: STRENGTH — same no-cap Rx benefit as Basic. Best Rx coverage among capped plans.

follow_up_specialist_visits.detail: Physician Office Visits: $20 copay (lower than Basic's $30), then URC up to Medical Maximum. 100% in-network. 90% OON first $10K (OOP max $1,000).
follow_up_specialist_visits.flag: Better than Basic: $20 copay vs $30, and OOP max $1,000 vs $2,000.

follow_up_imaging.detail: Covered as eligible medical expenses. Same coinsurance as outpatient. No imaging sub-limit.

strengths: Rx covered at URC with no sub-limit
weaknesses: PT still capped at $500
Home Nursing, DME, OT, Speech, Rehab unconfirmed
Cannot renew beyond 364 days

physical_therapy.best: Patriot_America_Plus / Patriot_America_Platinum — covered at URC, no visit cap
physical_therapy.moderate: CoverAmerica_Gold (no visit cap, 80% OON) / SC_Travel_Medical_Choice ($500 cap) / Safe_Travels_USA_Comprehensive ($500 cap) / INF_Elite_X ($500 cap) / VisitorSecure ($480 max at $40/visit)

needs_manual_review: All other plans — not confirmed in evidence

prescription_refills.best: SC_Travel_Medical_Basic / SC_Travel_Medical_Choice — URC up to plan max, no sub-limit
prescription_refills.strong: Patriot_America_Plus / Patriot_America_Platinum — URC, $250K sub-limit on high plans
prescription_refills.moderate: CoverAmerica_Gold (80% OON) / Safe_Travels_USA_Comprehensive ($250K cap on high plans)
prescription_refills.poor: INF_Elite_X ($500 confirmed for pre-ex; new-condition Rx ambiguous)
prescription_refills.worst: VisitorSecure — $150-$300 fixed cap total for entire trip

needs_manual_review: ALL 9 PLANS — no Certificate-level evidence for rehabilitation facility benefit in any plan
rehabilitation_facility.note: Rehabilitation facility coverage is a critical gap across all plans. None of the 9 Certificates explicitly confirmed this benefit.

occupational_and_speech_therapy.note: OT and Speech Therapy are consistently absent from traveler insurance Certificates. Likely excluded or not a standard benefit.

open_items.description: OT and Speech Therapy coverage not confirmed for any plan. Request full Certificate exclusion lists for all 9 plans.
Rehabilitation facility coverage not confirmed for any plan. IMG plans mention Extended Care Facility pre-certification — implies covered. Obtain explicit benefit schedule.
Home Nursing confirmed only for IMG plans. CoverAmerica Gold, Safe Travels, Seven Corners, INF all need Certificate verification.
DME confirmed only for IMG plans. Remaining 7 plans need verification.
INF Elite X outpatient Rx for new conditions (non-pre-ex) not confirmed. $500 cap confirmed only for pre-existing Rx sub-limit.
